raw_consumer_statement:
I'm here to complain about the way that Citi bank resolves credit card charge disputes. Citibank makes it unreasonably difficult to dispute charges so that their customers eventually give up. If they can charge XXXX  % APR, then they should provide the financial services they advertise. 

I bought a couch on XX/XX/2024 and never received it. After talking to the merchant, my only option was a partial refund, which meant losing > {$700.00}. I found it unfair to pay {$700.00} for something I never received, so I decided to dispute my charge with my credit card company. 

When you dispute the charge with Citi, they immediately put money back on the card, to give the customer immediate satisfaction. The immediate response makes you feel like the issue is resolved, but it isn't. After several weeks, they email you a PDF form and ask you to hand write more information about your dispute and physically mail it to their support centers. 

This is where most customers give up. This process should be much faster and should not involve any physical mail. Citibank does not have an advertised customer support email. They only have phone numbers where callers wait in queues for hours, or physical mailing addresses. They purposely make themselves difficult to contact so they can avoid providing the services they advertise. 

I've attached a screenshot of the form from another dispute they asked me to fill out and mail back as an example. I can't attach the entire document because it has personal financial information on it, so the screenshot is the best I can do. Notice that they ask me to mail the response to a XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX, XXXX XXXX, South Dakota instead of emailing them.
